title: My weekly call with my grandfather
summary: A short reflection on a small ritual of staying in touch across distance.
topic: My weekly call with my grandfather
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a weekly call or visit you have with one family member. Describe what you usually talk about and why the call matters. Use present simple for habits and going-to future for plans.
hintStarters: Every Sunday evening at about seven, I…
We usually talk about small things like…
Next month, I am going to…
sampleText: Every Sunday evening at seven o'clock, I sit on my sofa with a cup of tea and make a video call to my grandfather. He lives in a small town near the coast, more than a thousand kilometres from my flat. When he answers, the camera is always too close to his face, and I can only see his nose and one eye. Then he laughs, moves back, and I see his whole armchair with the same yellow blanket he has used for years. We do not talk about anything important. He tells me what the weather is like, what he ate for lunch, and which neighbours he met at the bakery. Sometimes he repeats the same story he told me the week before. I do not stop him, because he is happy when he tells it. Before we say goodbye, he always says, 'Don't work too hard,' even though he worked twelve hours a day for most of his life. Next month, I am going to take a train to see him in person. I plan to bring a notebook and write down his stories properly, before the day comes when there is no Sunday call to make.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Every Sunday evening at seven o'clock
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Mỗi tối Chủ Nhật lúc bảy giờ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the camera is always too close to his face
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: máy quay luôn quá sát mặt ông

sampleAnnotations.phrase: we do not talk about anything important
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chúng tôi không nói về điều gì quan trọng
kể lại cùng một câu chuyện

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he is happy when he tells it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ông hạnh phúc khi kể nó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I am going to take a train

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ghi chép lại câu chuyện của ông đàng hoàng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for routines
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng present simple cho thói quen và sự thật hiện tại: 'I sit on my sofa…', 'He tells me what the weather is like.' Thường đi kèm trạng từ như 'every', 'always', 'sometimes'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use present simple for routines and current facts: 'I sit on my sofa…', 'He tells me what the weather is like.' Often paired with adverbs like 'every', 'always', 'sometimes'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Going to' for planned future
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Be going to + V' diễn tả kế hoạch đã quyết định: 'Next month, I am going to take a train to see him.' Khác với will dùng cho quyết định ngay tại chỗ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Be going to + verb' expresses a decided plan: 'Next month, I am going to take a train to see him.' Different from 'will', which is used for on-the-spot decisions.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi tối Chủ Nhật lúc bảy giờ, tôi ngồi trên ghế sofa với một tách trà và gọi video cho ông tôi. Ông sống ở một thị trấn nhỏ gần biển, cách căn hộ của tôi hơn một nghìn cây số. Khi ông bắt máy, máy quay luôn quá sát mặt ông, và tôi chỉ thấy được mũi và một con mắt. Sau đó ông cười, lùi ra xa, và tôi thấy cả chiếc ghế bành với cùng chiếc chăn vàng ông đã dùng nhiều năm. Chúng tôi không nói về điều gì quan trọng. Ông kể cho tôi thời tiết thế nào, ông đã ăn gì trưa, và ông đã gặp người hàng xóm nào ở tiệm bánh. Đôi khi ông kể lại cùng một câu chuyện ông đã kể tuần trước. Tôi không ngắt lời, vì ông hạnh phúc khi kể nó. Trước khi chúng tôi nói tạm biệt, ông luôn nói, 'Đừng làm việc quá sức,' mặc dù ông đã làm việc mười hai giờ mỗi ngày trong gần như cả cuộc đời. Tháng tới, tôi sẽ đi tàu đến thăm ông trực tiếp. Tôi dự định mang theo một cuốn sổ và ghi chép lại câu chuyện của ông đàng hoàng, trước khi ngày không còn cuộc gọi Chủ Nhật nào nữa.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present-simple sentence for a routine.
options: Every Sunday evening, I am sitting on my sofa.
Every Sunday evening, I sit on my sofa.
Every Sunday evening, I sat on my sofa.
Every Sunday evening, I have sat on my sofa.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Every Sunday evening' signals a routine → present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct 'going to' for a plan.
options: Next month, I am go to take a train.
Next month, I am going to take a train.
Next month, I will going to take a train.
Next month, I going to take a train.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Be + going to + base verb' is the planned-future structure.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is correct word order?
options: He tells me always what the weather is like.
He always tells me what the weather is like.
He tells always me what the weather is like.
Always he tells me what the weather is like.
mcQuestions.explanation: Frequency adverb 'always' goes between subject and main verb.

options: to lose someone's number
to feel sad because someone is not with you
to forget someone's face
to walk past someone
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Miss someone' = feel sad about their absence.

options: He tells me what is the weather like.
He tells me what the weather is like.
He tells me what is like the weather.
He tells me what the weather like is.
mcQuestions.explanation: In embedded questions we keep subject-verb order: 'what the weather is like'.
mcQuestions.hint: Embedded question keeps statement word order.

options: Don't work too hard, even though he worked twelve hours a day.
Don't work too hard, even though he works twelve hours a day.
Don't work too hard, even though he is working twelve hours a day.
Don't work too hard, even though he has worked twelve hours a day.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'worked' fits 'for most of his life' (finished context).

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means 'something that happens once a week'?
options: weekly
monthly
daily
yearly

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence with 'before'.
options: Before we say goodbye, he always says, 'Don't work too hard.'
Before we will say goodbye, he always says, 'Don't work too hard.'
Before we are saying goodbye, he always says, 'Don't work too hard.'
Before we said goodbye, he always say, 'Don't work too hard.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Time clauses in present routines stay in present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct relative-meaning option for 'in person'.
options: by phone only
face to face, physically present
with a personal message
by writing a letter
make a video call
do a video call
take a video call
give a video call

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence using 'plan to + verb'.
options: I plan to bringing a notebook.
I plan to bring a notebook.
I plan bring a notebook.
I plan for bring a notebook.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Plan to' is followed by the base verb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think a weekly small call keeps a relationship alive in a way that occasional long visits cannot? Why or why not?
